records.ety: [L. publicanus: cf. F. publicain. See Public.]

defs: A farmer of the taxes and public revenues; hence, a collector of toll or tribute. The inferior officers of this class were often oppressive in their exactions, and were regarded with great detestation.
quotes: As Jesus at meat . . . many publicans and sinners came and sat down with him and his disciples.
How like a fawning publican he looks!

defs: The keeper of an inn or public house; one licensed to retail beer, spirits, or wine.
